label: CSV Processor
description: Parse CSV text into records (objects keyed by header) or rows (string arrays). V1 ships text-mode only; binary/file mode is a follow-up after the binary data plane lands.

settings.name: csv
settings.label: CSV text
settings.description: Raw CSV text. Template against {{trigger.body.X}} / {{steps.Y.Z}} to pull from an upstream node.

settings.name: hasHeader
settings.label: First row is header
settings.description: When true, the first row supplies column names for records mode. When false, output is rows[] regardless of outputAs.

settings.name: delimiter
settings.label: Delimiter
settings.description: Field delimiter. Use "\t" for TSV. Default is comma.

settings.name: outputAs
settings.label: Output shape
settings.description: records = array of {col: value} objects (requires hasHeader). rows = array of string[].

ai.shortDescription: Parse CSV text into records or rows.
ai.whenToUse: Place downstream of an http_request returning CSV, or anywhere you have inline CSV text and want structured rows.
ai.examplePrompt: parse the body of the http_request as CSV with a header and return records
ai.promptHints: Output: {{steps.X.records}} (array of objects) or {{steps.X.rows}} (array of arrays). {{steps.X.columns}} is the header list. Use iterator downstream to loop over rows/records.

outputAssertions.name: has_envelope
outputAssertions.errorMessage: csv_processor output is missing the {outputAs, count} envelope.
